---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 4bc41d61669abb40bf5bdaff86f377fffb8d30cf
findings:
  - { id: judged-f-1, kind: judged, text: "spec/worktree-manager dc-3 narrows the deleted-reclamation signal to local-only (branch absent under refs/heads/design/*), while parent dc-4 binds reclamation to 'the ratified gc signals', whose ratified deleted signal is fetch --prune then neither-local-nor-remote. Under dc-3 a worktree whose local branch is deleted but whose same-named remote-tracking ref persists is reclaimed even though the ratified signal would not fire — a substantive divergence from parent dc-4 carried only as prose disclosure, with no supersedes/exempts edge declared against workbench-directory dc-4 (the spec itself concedes dc-4 is a valid fragment target and declines the edge on R4-I-12 proportionality grounds, which is a process argument, not an edge). (confidence 0.70)", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory, disposition: exempt, note: "Human disposition (design review): the divergence is real and correctly flagged, and its target (parent dc-4) is a valid exempts-edge fragment — but a spec-level exempts edge on this story would trip R4-I-12's stub-match test, which disqualifies ANY exempts edge unconditionally and forces full review regardless of the substance's narrowness. That cost is disproportionate for an implementation-scoped signal choice for a target (managed worktrees) the parent decision's 'ratified gc signals' phrase never contemplated, since managed worktrees postdate it entirely. The reasoning (worktree-manager dc-3: a managed worktree is bound to one specific local branch, dc-1, and cannot be resurrected from a surviving remote-tracking ref without an explicit act dc-1 already forbids) is recorded in the story spec's own prose for any future reviewer to escalate on its merits; this disposition is the design-review-time record of that judgment, not a claim that no divergence exists." }
  - { id: judged-f-2, kind: judged, text: "spec/worktree-manager dc-4 adds a second keep-reason ('kept: in use by pid N' when the dc-2 lockfile is held live) beyond parent dc-4's single named exception (uncommitted changes), so gc can decline to reclaim a merged/deleted worktree that parent dc-4 defines as reclaimable. The spec acknowledges this is 'a keep-reason beyond parent dc-4's single named exception' yet declares no exempts edge against workbench-directory dc-4, disclosing in prose only; the transient-race narrowness argument mitigates but does not remove the undeclared contradiction with the parent's reclamation model. (confidence 0.55)", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory, disposition: exempt, note: "Human disposition (design review): same proportionality reasoning as judged-f-1 — parent dc-4 is a valid exempts target, but declaring the edge would force full review under R4-I-12 for a narrow, transient safety margin (worktree-manager dc-2/dc-4: the lock is held only for the duration of a single git-worktree-mutating operation, so the skip is bounded to a single race window, not an extended or indefinite hold like the parent's own dirty-worktree exception). The reasoning is recorded in the story spec's own prose; this disposition records that it was seen, considered, and accepted at design review rather than silently dropped." }
  - { id: judged-f-3, kind: judged, text: "spec/worktree-manager dc-3 makes managed worktrees reclaim-eligible the instant a signal fires, with no retention_days grace period, while parent dc-4 invokes 'the ratified gc signals' generically — and the ratified gc behavior in verdi-store-layout applies a retention_days horizon to merged/deleted refs. If parent dc-4's generic invocation is read as importing the ratified horizon along with the signals, instant reclamation diverges from it with no declared edge; the spec argues the horizon's applicability is genuinely ambiguous and store-layout cannot carry an edge target, but the parent-feature fragment dc-4 could, and none was declared. (confidence 0.40)", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory, disposition: exempt, note: "Human disposition (design review): the retention_days applicability is genuinely ambiguous in verdi-store-layout's own text (its config comment is worded generally, but that component spec predates managed worktrees and cannot have settled the question either way). Read narrowly, parent dc-4 only invokes 'the ratified gc signals' (merged/deleted, dirty-is-kept) by reference, not the retention horizon specifically; read broadly, this is the same proportionality situation as judged-f-1/f-2. Recorded here rather than via a spec-level exempts edge for the identical R4-I-12 stub-match reason; a future revision may unify the retention policy across derived-cache and managed-worktree reclamation, or may deliberately keep them separate, without breaking this story's contract either way." }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/workbench-directory#dc-1, spec/workbench-directory#dc-2, spec/workbench-directory#dc-3, spec/workbench-directory#dc-4, spec/workbench-directory#dc-5, spec/worktree-manager#dc-1, spec/worktree-manager#dc-2, spec/worktree-manager#dc-3, spec/worktree-manager#dc-4, spec/worktree-manager#dc-5] }
digest: sha256:9435d5ce9bc7f531fb7efad1a675b95a3dd32afbc2474fb706b8bdfe63636744
integrity: sha256:21e2389b3d5c862990279aa5594a21256d399a4314af8c92ed1d42acfe74382a
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"f-1\",\"text\":\"spec/worktree-manager dc-3 narrows the deleted-reclamation signal to local-only (branch absent under refs/heads/design/*), while parent dc-4 binds reclamation to 'the ratified gc signals', whose ratified deleted signal is fetch --prune then neither-local-nor-remote. Under dc-3 a worktree whose local branch is deleted but whose same-named remote-tracking ref persists is reclaimed even though the ratified signal would not fire — a substantive divergence from parent dc-4 carried only as prose disclosure, with no supersedes/exempts edge declared against workbench-directory dc-4 (the spec itself concedes dc-4 is a valid fragment target and declines the edge on R4-I-12 proportionality grounds, which is a process argument, not an edge).\",\"confidence\":0.7,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-directory\"},{\"id\":\"f-2\",\"text\":\"spec/worktree-manager dc-4 adds a second keep-reason ('kept: in use by pid N' when the dc-2 lockfile is held live) beyond parent dc-4's single named exception (uncommitted changes), so gc can decline to reclaim a merged/deleted worktree that parent dc-4 defines as reclaimable. The spec acknowledges this is 'a keep-reason beyond parent dc-4's single named exception' yet declares no exempts edge against workbench-directory dc-4, disclosing in prose only; the transient-race narrowness argument mitigates but does not remove the undeclared contradiction with the parent's reclamation model.\",\"confidence\":0.55,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-directory\"},{\"id\":\"f-3\",\"text\":\"spec/worktree-manager dc-3 makes managed worktrees reclaim-eligible the instant a signal fires, with no retention_days grace period, while parent dc-4 invokes 'the ratified gc signals' generically — and the ratified gc behavior in verdi-store-layout applies a retention_days horizon to merged/deleted refs. If parent dc-4's generic invocation is read as importing the ratified horizon along with the signals, instant reclamation diverges from it with no declared edge; the spec argues the horizon's applicability is genuinely ambiguous and store-layout cannot carry an edge target, but the parent-feature fragment dc-4 could, and none was declared.\",\"confidence\":0.4,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-directory\"}]}\n```" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v1, inputs: [spec/worktree-manager@4bc41d61669abb40bf5bdaff86f377fffb8d30cf], digest: sha256:9435d5ce9bc7f531fb7efad1a675b95a3dd32afbc2474fb706b8bdfe63636744, integrity: sha256:21e2389b3d5c862990279aa5594a21256d399a4314af8c92ed1d42acfe74382a }
---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-f-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: spec/worktree-manager dc-3 narrows the deleted-reclamation signal to local-only (branch absent under refs/heads/design/*), while parent dc-4 binds reclamation to 'the ratified gc signals', whose ratified deleted signal is fetch --prune then neither-local-nor-remote. Under dc-3 a worktree whose local branch is deleted but whose same-named remote-tracking ref persists is reclaimed even though the ratified signal would not fire — a substantive divergence from parent dc-4 carried only as prose disclosure, with no supersedes/exempts edge declared against workbench-directory dc-4 (the spec itself concedes dc-4 is a valid fragment target and declines the edge on R4-I-12 proportionality grounds, which is a process argument, not an edge). (confidence 0.70)
- **judged-f-2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: spec/worktree-manager dc-4 adds a second keep-reason ('kept: in use by pid N' when the dc-2 lockfile is held live) beyond parent dc-4's single named exception (uncommitted changes), so gc can decline to reclaim a merged/deleted worktree that parent dc-4 defines as reclaimable. The spec acknowledges this is 'a keep-reason beyond parent dc-4's single named exception' yet declares no exempts edge against workbench-directory dc-4, disclosing in prose only; the transient-race narrowness argument mitigates but does not remove the undeclared contradiction with the parent's reclamation model. (confidence 0.55)
- **judged-f-3** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: spec/worktree-manager dc-3 makes managed worktrees reclaim-eligible the instant a signal fires, with no retention_days grace period, while parent dc-4 invokes 'the ratified gc signals' generically — and the ratified gc behavior in verdi-store-layout applies a retention_days horizon to merged/deleted refs. If parent dc-4's generic invocation is read as importing the ratified horizon along with the signals, instant reclamation diverges from it with no declared edge; the spec argues the horizon's applicability is genuinely ambiguous and store-layout cannot carry an edge target, but the parent-feature fragment dc-4 could, and none was declared. (confidence 0.40)
